title: Boat Rental in Cartagena: Costs, Seasons and Tips 2026
html:
A day on the water in Cartagena starts at the Real Club de Regatas marina, where boat rental in Cartagena ranges from roughly EUR 250 for a small motorboat to EUR 1,500 for a crewed yacht. BoatHire24 lists verified boats here, ready to explore the bay and nearby coves.

Where to set off in Cartagena

Cartagena sits on Spain's Costa Cálida, and its sheltered harbour gives you several genuine launch points within a short hop of open water. Most charters leave from one of these spots, then turn towards the headlands and bays that line the Murcia coast.

  • Puerto de Cartagena: the working and leisure harbour beneath the old town, with quayside pick-ups near the marina.
  • Real Club de Regatas de Cartagena: the historic sailing club, a common departure point for skippered day trips.
  • Yacht Port Cartagena: the modern marina basin used by many private boats and charter operators.
  • Cabo Tinoso: a dramatic cape west of the city, popular for clear-water anchoring.
  • Isla de las Palomas and Cala Cortina: a close-in island and a small city beach cove, both easy short runs from the port.

Which boat to rent in Cartagena?

The right boat depends on group size and how far you want to roam along the Murcia coast. For a relaxed bay day a small motorboat is plenty, while larger groups and longer runs to Cabo Tinoso suit a flybridge cruiser or crewed yacht.

  1. Small motorboat: best for couples and short coastal hops, seats up to 6, from EUR 250 a day.
  2. RIB or speedboat: best for reaching distant coves quickly, seats up to 8, from EUR 350.
  3. Flybridge motor yacht: a roomy option such as the Fairline Targa 43 charter page, ideal for families wanting shade and a galley, seats up to 10, from EUR 900.
  4. Sailing yacht: best for a quieter day under canvas, seats up to 8, from EUR 600.
  5. Crewed luxury yacht: best for celebrations with a skipper aboard, seats up to 12, from EUR 1,500.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Cartagena?

Most day rentals in Cartagena fall between EUR 250 and EUR 1,500, depending on the boat and whether a skipper is included. The bands below give a clearer picture before you compare dates.

  • A shared coastal tour seat costs from about EUR 35 per person in EUR.
  • A small motorboat for a half-day runs from roughly EUR 180 in EUR.
  • A catamaran for a full day starts near EUR 1,100 in EUR.
  • A crewed yacht with skipper begins around EUR 1,500 per day in EUR, fuel often charged separately.

You can compare live prices and skippered charters in Cartagena directly on the listing pages, which show what each boat includes before you commit. Costa Cálida quite literally means warm coast, and the calm summer sea here keeps shorter rentals very accessible.

When is the best season to rent a boat in Cartagena?

The high season runs from June to September, when warm, settled weather makes the bay ideal for a full day afloat. Spring and early autumn stay pleasant and quieter, though afternoon breezes can build.

Local tip: book morning departures in July and August. The Levante wind, an easterly that funnels along this coast, often freshens by mid-afternoon, so an early start gives you the calmest water for swimming around Cala Cortina and Isla de las Palomas.

Spain follows the principle that larger or faster boats require a recognised licence, while many smaller motorboats can be hired without one or taken with a skipper. The operator confirms the exact requirements for each boat, so always check the listing before you book.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 250 to EUR 1,500 per day
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Typical trip length: 4 to 8 hours
  • Capacity: 6 to 12 guests depending on boat
  • Currency: EUR
  • Nearest open cove: Cala Cortina, under 2 nautical miles from the port

Questions about boat rental in Cartagena

Do I need a licence to rent a boat in Cartagena?

For many small motorboats you do not need a licence in Spain, and any boat can be booked with a skipper instead. The operator states the exact rule for each listing, so confirm before paying.

How far can I sail in a day from Cartagena?

A comfortable day trip covers the coast from Cabo Tinoso to Cabo de Palos, a stretch of roughly 20 nautical miles, with time to anchor and swim. Faster boats reach the outer capes more easily.

Can beginners enjoy a day on the water here?

Beginners are well served because skippered charters mean an experienced captain handles the boat. You simply relax, swim and take in the coastline while the crew manages navigation and anchoring throughout the day.

What should I bring aboard?

Bring sun protection, a hat, soft-soled shoes, swimwear and water, since the Cartagena sun is strong in summer. Most boats supply safety gear, and many include a fridge or cool box for your own snacks.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Cartagena or list your own, and the 2026 season is already filling for warm summer dates. Browse listings, message owners and reserve through the BoatHire24 marketplace for a smooth day afloat.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Cartagena and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
